Home / Services / Storm Work

Storm Work

Rapid-response storm cleanup, drainage restoration, and property protection when you need it most.

Get a Free Estimate

Overview

Southwest Ohio’s storms can hit hard — high winds, heavy rain, ice, and tornadic activity can leave properties damaged and inaccessible in a matter of hours. When a storm strikes your property, Earthwise Excavating provides the rapid-response excavation and cleanup services you need to get things back to normal.

Our heavy equipment can handle what pickup trucks and chainsaws can’t: massive fallen trees, washed-out embankments, collapsed culverts, and blocked access roads. We work quickly and efficiently to clear the damage, restore drainage, and protect your property from further deterioration.

What’s Included

  • Rapid Assessment — We get to your property quickly to evaluate the damage and prioritize the most critical work.
  • Tree & Debris Clearing — Heavy equipment removes large fallen trees, branches, and structural debris that blocks access and threatens structures.
  • Driveway & Road Restoration — We rebuild washed-out drives, clear blocked roads, and restore access to your property.
  • Drainage Repair — Damaged culverts, collapsed ditches, and blocked drainage systems are repaired or replaced to prevent ongoing water damage.
  • Erosion Stabilization — We grade and stabilize areas damaged by water erosion to prevent the damage from spreading.

Our Process

  1. Emergency Contact — Call us at (513) 212-7585. We prioritize storm calls and will get a crew to you as fast as possible.
  2. Damage Assessment — We evaluate the full scope of damage and create an action plan, addressing safety hazards first.
  3. Cleanup & Restoration — Our crew clears debris, restores access, repairs drainage, and stabilizes any erosion damage.
  4. Documentation & Prevention — We provide documentation for insurance claims and recommend improvements to reduce future storm vulnerability.

FAQ

Common Questions About Storm Work

How quickly can you respond after a storm? +

We prioritize emergency storm calls and do our best to be on-site within 24-48 hours of contact, depending on the severity and volume of damage in the area. For situations involving immediate safety hazards like blocked roads or threatened structures, we work to respond as quickly as possible. Call us at (513) 212-7585 — we'll get back to you fast.

What types of storm damage can you handle? +

We handle fallen tree and debris removal, washed-out driveways and roads, erosion damage to hillsides and embankments, blocked or damaged drainage systems, damaged culverts, and property access restoration. Essentially, if it involves moving earth, clearing debris, or restoring drainage, we can help.

Will my insurance cover storm damage repair? +

Many homeowner and farm insurance policies cover storm damage to property improvements like driveways, drainage systems, and structural foundations. We can provide detailed estimates and documentation to support your insurance claim. We recommend contacting your insurance provider as soon as possible after storm damage occurs.

Can you help prevent future storm damage? +

Absolutely. After addressing the immediate damage, we often identify drainage improvements, erosion control measures, and grading adjustments that can reduce vulnerability to future storms. Prevention work is always more cost-effective than emergency repairs, and it's a core part of our conservation-focused approach.

Ready to Get Started?

Contact us today for a free consultation about storm work.
